change_comments.message:
package are to much based on teh types of class but shoudl be based on usecases instead. As discused lets drop teh 'facadsa' and organise like so:


cmsubscriptioncache
-cache
-models
-ncmp 
--ncmpInEventConsumer
--ncmpOutProduducer
--CmNotificationSubscriptionHandler
--CmNotificationSubscriptionNcmpOutEventPublishingTask
--CmNotificationSubscriptionComparator
--CmNotificationSubscriptionNcmpOutEventMapper
-dmi
--dmiInProducer
--dmiOutConsumer
--CmNotificationSubscriptionDmiInEventMapper
-utils
--persistenceService

Structrue looks fine, Well done!

Main problem is that IntelliJ did NOT change map names and mocks and a few legacy inconsistent names, You just have to do them al manually...
I think I highlight most of them but make suer to do a good self review in Nordix when you think you are done :-)

a lot of hard work done last night.
The main issue is this the name of map:
Map<String, DmiCmSubscriptionDetails> dmiSubscriptionPerDmi

I think this name is OK (teh word detaisl isn't really needed), you start using it consistently in a big class using it.

But then you start being inconsistent and calling it
-dmiSubscriptionDetailsPerDmi
-dmiSubscriptionDetailsMap

Just make sure you always use the same name in the production code and test code!

Maybe to a search of 'Map<String, DmiCmSubscriptionDetails>' to ensure you get all te instances of it. I actually only review the classed that changed in the last commit and try to make them all but it might be in classes you haven't changed too...
